name: Motor insurance add-on covers explained
description: User wants to understand which add-on covers are worth buying

input.message: I'm renewing my car insurance. The insurer is offering zero depreciation, engine protect, roadside assistance, return to invoice, and consumables cover as add-ons. Which ones are actually worth the extra premium?

notes: Must recommend zero-dep as most important (saves 40-50% on parts claim). Engine protect for new/expensive cars. RSA for long-distance drivers. Return to invoice for cars < 3 years. Must be objective, not push all add-ons.
